entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop being studied for drought resistance and productivity.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: The major abiotic constraint affecting groundnut productivity and quality worldwide.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop output affected by drought.

entities.name: QUALITY
entities.description: The characteristic of the crop output affected by drought.

entities.name: EMPIRICAL SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding approach for drought resistance based on kernel yield and traits of local adaptation.

entities.name: KERNEL YIELD
entities.description: A primary trait used in empirical selection and a measure of selection efficiency.

entities.name: LOCAL ADAPTATION
entities.description: Traits used as a basis for the empirical selection approach.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Measurable surrogates associated with drought tolerance used in selection schemes.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL TRAIT-BASED SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding approach that integrates physiological traits or their surrogates into selection schemes.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the collaborating institutes and field trials were located.

entities.name: RESPONSE TO SELECTION
entities.description: The genetic concept used to estimate the selection efficiency of different methods.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: An environmental grouping where physiological trait-based selection showed higher relative response.

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: An environmental grouping where physiological trait-based selection showed lower relative response.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIANCE
entities.description: A factor contributing to the relative response to selection in different seasons.

entities.name: GENOTYPE BY ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: A factor (G x E) influencing the relative response to selection.

entities.name: HERITABILITY
entities.description: A factor (h2) influencing the relative response to selection.

entities.name: SOIL-WATER AVAILABILITY
entities.description: A factor used to cluster environments to test selection method superiority.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION
entities.description: A physiological trait that was higher in empirical selections but potentially disadvantageous in water-deficient environments.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A physiological trait measured to compare selection methods.

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: A physiological trait measured to compare selection methods.

entities.name: WATER-DEFICIENT ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Environments where high transpiration is not an advantage for yield.

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of genotypes to be efficient water utilisers.

entities.name: PHOTOSYNTHATE PARTITIONING
entities.description: The ability of genotypes to partition photosynthates into economic yield.

entities.name: BIOTECHNOLOGICAL TOOLS
entities.description: Tools being explored to increase the efficiency of physiological trait-based drought resistance breeding.
